commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Google Chat native approval cards, opaque callback-token handling, card-click webhook normalization, message update support for `cardsV2`, focused tests, and docs for exec/plugin approval routing.

PR surface: Source +981, Tests +744, Docs +10. Total +1735 across 18 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR rather than a bug report. Source inspection verifies current main is text-only for Google Chat approvals, and the PR body reports a real Google Chat DM proof path for the new behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The protected `maintainer` label plus the approval callback security surface make this a human review item, not an automated repair candidate.

target.title: feat(googlechat): add native approval cards
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds native Google Chat approval cards for exec and plugin approval requests that originate from Google Chat spaces or threads.
- Uses opaque server-side action tokens for Google Chat `cardsV2` button callbacks while preserving `/approve <id> <decision>` in message text as the fallback.
- Normalizes standard Chat and Workspace add-on card-click webhook payloads, verifies add-on system ID tokens, and updates delivered approval messages after resolution or expiry.
- Documents the Google Chat approval-card behavior and advanced exec/plugin approval routing notes.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related Spec 24.8: Google Chat native approval cards.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Requested by maintainer in the Codex task thread.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Google Chat exec and plugin approvals render as native cards and resolve through Google Chat button clicks.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw dev profile with a real Google Chat DM to the OpenClaw app, local gateway on port 19001 behind a temporary Cloudflare quick tunnel, and Arc CDP against the signed-in `kevin@dendron.so` Google Chat session.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the dev-profile gateway with the Google Chat webhook routed through the tunnel, sent an exec approval prompt and a plugin approval prompt from Google Chat, clicked the native `Allow Once` card buttons in Google Chat, and verified gateway resolution logs for both approval IDs.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Exec approval `ded46107-253c-43e9-8e86-b3f597ba01e7` resolved with `allow-once`; plugin approval `plugin:52188daa-b8a3-4bb3-822f-3091adf7117b` resolved with `allow-once`; gateway logged the Google Chat actor as `users/105390401933428738525`. Local Showboat proof summary verified at `.mem/main/proofs/demo-35-dev-googlechat-approval-cards/raw/showboat-summary.md`.
- Observed result after fix: Both approval prompts posted native Google Chat cards, both button clicks were accepted only for the configured stable `users/<id>` approver, and both approvals resolved through the gateway.
- What was not tested: A persistent production Google Chat app URL; live proof used a temporary Cloudflare tunnel for the local dev callback.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Computer Use could not attach to the Arc window during proof, so the browser click path used Arc CDP for the already signed-in Google Chat surface.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, Google Chat approval prompts were text-only and card button callbacks were not wired into approval resolution.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/googlechat/src`
- `pnpm build`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- `uvx showboat verify .mem/main/proofs/demo-35-dev-googlechat-approval-cards/raw/showboat-summary.md`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added Google Chat native approval capability, runtime delivery, card token, and card-click resolver tests.
- Added webhook normalization coverage for Workspace add-on card-click payloads.
- Updated send/update API tests for `cardsV2` payloads and update masks.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Google Chat card clicks were not represented in the webhook event path or bound to approval resolution, so native card approvals could not complete an exec or plugin approval.

If no test was added, why not?

Tests were added for the changed runtime and webhook behavior.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

What is the highest-risk area?

Approval authorization and callback token handling for native Google Chat card actions.

How is that risk mitigated?

Callbacks carry opaque action tokens only, token bindings check account, space, message, expiry, allowed decision, and in-flight state, and actor authorization reuses the existing Google Chat approver allowlist adapter based on stable `users/<id>` principals.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI is pending after PR creation; live dev-profile proof is complete.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

No PR comments exist yet.
target.author: kevinslin
